<think> To answer this question, I need to recall the timeline of William Whitelaw's career in the Conservative Party. I know that William Whitelaw was a prominent figure in the party, serving as Deputy Leader from 1975 to 1979. I also recall that Edward Heath formed the first Shadow Cabinet in 1965, and the second Shadow Cabinet in 1970, when he became the Leader of the Opposition. I need to determine which of these two Shadow Cabinets came before Whitelaw's tenure as Deputy Leader. Since Whitelaw became Deputy Leader in 1975, I can infer that the second Shadow Cabinet of Edward Heath, which was formed in 1970, came before Whitelaw's tenure. Therefore, Whitelaw was Deputy Leader of the Conservative Party after the formation of the second Shadow Cabinet of Edward Heath.</think>
<answer> after </answer>
